cards.front: What is the main source of fluoride in drinking water?
cards.back: Fluoride from water supplies, particularly from groundwater, and from the addition of fluoride to community water systems

cards.front: What is the main health concern associated with excessive fluoride in drinking water?
cards.back: Dental and skeletal fluorosis, as well as potential neurological damage

cards.front: Which group of people is most vulnerable to the effects of excessive fluoride in drinking water?
cards.back: Children and pregnant women, whose developing bones and teeth are more susceptible to fluoride's effects

cards.front: What is the optimal level of fluoride in drinking water, according to the World Health Organization?
What is the main cause of fluoride contamination in drinking water?
cards.back: Agricultural runoff, industrial waste, and mining activities
